message:
fix(fcp-streaming): require data frame to reset backoff (br-f46kk)

crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs ReconnectingWsStream::
note_message_received previously reset the reconnect budget on any
non-Close frame after the br-hjaej fix, which was still too permissive.

Ping and Pong control frames reach note_message_received via
Ok(Some(WsMessage::Ping(..))) / Pong(..) from recv(). An adversarial
peer that completes the WS handshake and immediately streams Pings
(or replays an unsolicited Pong) therefore reset the retry budget to
zero, preserving the reconnect-storm hole that hjaej closed under a
different control frame.

A genuine ping/pong round-trip is NOT observable at this call site —
we see inbound control frames but not the matching outbound Ping we
would need to verify reachability. The safe invariant is that ONLY
data-carrying application frames (Text or Binary) count as proof of
a healthy session.

## Fix

Tighten the reset predicate in note_message_received:

    if !message.is_close() { reset }

becomes

    if !(message.is_text() || message.is_binary()) { return; }

All control frames (Close, Ping, Pong) now preserve the retry
budget. The reset gate stays armed so the first genuine data frame
on a later connect can still clear it.

## Regression tests (3 new)

  reconnect_stream_ping_frame_does_not_reset_backoff
  reconnect_stream_pong_frame_does_not_reset_backoff
  reconnect_stream_data_frame_after_control_burst_resets_backoff

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-streaming --lib reconnect_stream
  -> 7 passed (4 pre-existing + 3 new).
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-streaming --lib
  -> 525 passed (was 522 + 3 new); 1 pre-existing flaky timing
     failure unrelated.

Closes flywheel_connectors-f46kk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
